lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਬੂਝੇ ਸਭੁ ਵਾਦਿ ਜੋਨੀ ਭਰਮਤੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: bin boojhe sabh vaad jonee bharamate |5|
lines.english: Without understanding, they are totally useless, and they wander in reincarnation. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਹਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਅਨੇਕ ਮਰਿ ਮਰਿ ਜਨਮਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: aaveh jaeh anek mar mar janamate |
lines.english: Many come and go; they die, and die again, and are reincarnated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑ ਕਉ ਭਏ ਦਇਆਲ ਤਿਨੑ ਸਾਧੂ ਸੰਗੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jina kau bhe deaal tina saadhoo sang bheaa |
lines.english: They alone join the Saadh Sangat, unto whom the Lord becomes Merciful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਤਿਨੑੀ ਜਨੀ ਜਪਿ ਲਇਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: amrit har kaa naam tinaee janee jap leaa |6|
lines.english: They chant and meditate on the Ambrosial Name of the Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਬੁਝਾਏ ਆਪਿ ਨੇੜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਹੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jis bujhaae aap nerraa tis he |7|
lines.english: But only that one, who understands his own self, sees God near at hand. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਸਰੁ ਨਾਹੀ ਦਾਤਾਰ ਆਪਣਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਦੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: visar naahee daataar aapanaa naam dehu |
lines.english: Never forget me, O Great Giver - please bless me with Your Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਜਹਿ ਕੋਟਿ ਅਸੰਖ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਅਨੰਤ ਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: khojeh kott asankh bahut anant ke |
lines.english: Uncounted millions, so many they are endless, search for Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਾ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਚਾਉ ਏਹੁ ॥੮॥੨॥੫॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: gun gaavaa din raat naanak chaau ehu |8|2|5|16|
lines.english: To sing Your Glorious Praises day and night - O Nanak, this is my heart-felt desire. ||8||2||5||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਸੂਹੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਕੁਚਜੀ ॥
lines.translit: raag soohee mahalaa 1 kuchajee |
lines.english: Raag Soohee, First Mehl, Kuchajee ~ The Ungraceful Bride:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਞੁ ਕੁਚਜੀ ਅੰਮਾਵਣਿ ਡੋਸੜੇ ਹਉ ਕਿਉ ਸਹੁ ਰਾਵਣਿ ਜਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: many kuchajee amaavan ddosarre hau kiau sahu raavan jaau jeeo |
lines.english: I am ungraceful and ill-mannered, full of endless faults. How can I go to enjoy my Husband Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਦੂ ਇਕਿ ਚੜੰਦੀਆ ਕਉਣੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਨਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: eik doo ik charrandeea kaun jaanai meraa naau jeeo |
lines.english: Each of His soul-brides is better than the rest - who even knows my name?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਗੁਣ ਮੰਞੁ ਨ ਆਵਨੀ ਹਉ ਕੈ ਜੀ ਦੋਸ ਧਰੇਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: se gun many na aavanee hau kai jee dos dhareo jeeo |
lines.english: I do not have their virtue - who can I blame for this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑੀ ਸਖੀ ਸਹੁ ਰਾਵਿਆ ਸੇ ਅੰਬੀ ਛਾਵੜੀਏਹਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: jinaee sakhee sahu raaviaa se anbee chhaavarreehi jeeo |
lines.english: Those brides who enjoy their Husband Lord are very blessed, resting in the shade of the mango tree.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਗੁਣ ਤੇਰੇ ਵਿਥਰਾ ਹਉ ਕਿਆ ਕਿਆ ਘਿਨਾ ਤੇਰਾ ਨਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa gun tere vitharaa hau kiaa kiaa ghinaa teraa naau jeeo |
lines.english: Which of Your Virtues, O Lord, should I speak of? Which of Your Names should I chant?

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਤੁ ਟੋਲਿ ਨ ਅੰਬੜਾ ਹਉ ਸਦ ਕੁਰਬਾਣੈ ਤੇਰੈ ਜਾਉ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: eikat ttol na anbarraa hau sad kurabaanai terai jaau jeeo |
lines.english: I cannot even reach one of Your Virtues. I am forever a sacrifice to You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਵਸਤੂ ਸਹਿ ਦਿਤੀਆ ਮੈ ਤਿਨੑ ਸਿਉ ਲਾਇਆ ਚਿਤੁ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: se vasatoo seh diteea mai tina siau laaeaa chit jeeo |
lines.english: My Husband Lord has blessed me with these things, and I have focused my thoughts on them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਇਨਾ ਰੁਪਾ ਰੰਗੁਲਾ ਮੋਤੀ ਤੈ ਮਾਣਿਕੁ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: sueinaa rupaa rangulaa motee tai maanik jeeo |
lines.english: Gold, silver, pearls and rubies are pleasing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਰ ਮਿਟੀ ਸੰਦੜੇ ਪਥਰ ਕੀਤੇ ਰਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: mandar mittee sandarre pathar keete raas jeeo |
lines.english: Palaces of brick and mud are built and decorated with stones;

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਏਨੀ ਟੋਲੀ ਭੁਲੀਅਸੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਕੰਤ ਨ ਬੈਠੀ ਪਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: hau enee ttolee bhuleeas tis kant na baitthee paas jeeo |
lines.english: I have been fooled by these decorations, and I do not sit near my Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਬਰਿ ਕੂੰਜਾ ਕੁਰਲੀਆ ਬਗ ਬਹਿਠੇ ਆਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: anbar koonjaa kuraleea bag bahitthe aae jeeo |
lines.english: The cranes shriek overhead in the sky, and the herons have come to rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਧਨ ਚਲੀ ਸਾਹੁਰੈ ਕਿਆ ਮੁਹੁ ਦੇਸੀ ਅਗੈ ਜਾਇ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: saa dhan chalee saahurai kiaa muhu desee agai jaae jeeo |
lines.english: The bride has gone to her father-in-law's house; in the world hereafter, what face will she show?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਤੀ ਸੁਤੀ ਝਾਲੁ ਥੀਆ ਭੁਲੀ ਵਾਟੜੀਆਸੁ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: sutee sutee jhaal theea bhulee vaattarreeaas jeeo |
lines.english: She kept sleeping as the day dawned; she forgot all about her journey.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਸਹ ਨਾਲਹੁ ਮੁਤੀਅਸੁ ਦੁਖਾ ਕੂੰ ਧਰੀਆਸੁ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: tai seh naalahu muteeas dukhaa koon dhareeaas jeeo |
lines.english: She separated herself from her Husband Lord, and now she suffers in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਗੁਣ ਮੈ ਸਭਿ ਅਵਗਣਾ ਇਕ ਨਾਨਕ ਕੀ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: tudh gun mai sabh avaganaa ik naanak kee aradaas jeeo |
lines.english: Virtue is in You, O Lord; I am totally without virtue. This is Nanak's only prayer:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਰਾਤੀ ਸੋਹਾਗਣੀ ਮੈ ਡੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਕਾਈ ਰਾਤਿ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sabh raatee sohaaganee mai ddohaagan kaaee raat jeeo |1|
lines.english: You give all Your nights to the virtuous soul-brides. I know I am unworthy, but isn't there a night for me as well? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤੂ ਤਾ ਮੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਤੂ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਮੇਰੀ ਰਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaa too taa mai sabh ko too saahib meree raas jeeo |
lines.english: When I have You, then I have everything. O my Lord and Master, You are my wealth and capital.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਹੀ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਸੁਚਜੀ ॥
lines.translit: soohee mahalaa 1 suchajee |
lines.english: Soohee, First Mehl, Suchajee ~ The Noble And Graceful Bride:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਹਉ ਸੁਖਿ ਵਸਾ ਤੂੰ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਾਬਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: tudh antar hau sukh vasaa toon antar saabaas jeeo |
lines.english: Within You, I abide in peace; within You, I am congratulated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਣੈ ਤਖਤਿ ਵਡਾਈਆ ਭਾਣੈ ਭੀਖ ਉਦਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaanai takhat vaddaaeea bhaanai bheekh udaas jeeo |
lines.english: By the Pleasure of Your Will, You bestow thrones and greatness. And by the Pleasure of Your Will, You make us beggars and wanderers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਣੈ ਥਲ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਰੁ ਵਹੈ ਕਮਲੁ ਫੁਲੈ ਆਕਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaanai thal sir sar vahai kamal fulai aakaas jeeo |
lines.english: By the Pleasure of Your Will, the ocean flows in the desert, and the lotus blossoms in the sky.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਣੈ ਭਵਜਲੁ ਲੰਘੀਐ ਭਾਣੈ ਮੰਝਿ ਭਰੀਆਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaanai bhavajal langheeai bhaanai manjh bhareeaas jeeo |
lines.english: By the Pleasure of Your Will, one crosses over the terrifying world-ocean; by the Pleasure of Your Will, he sinks down into it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਣੈ ਸੋ ਸਹੁ ਰੰਗੁਲਾ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਰਤਾ ਗੁਣਤਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaanai so sahu rangulaa sifat rataa gunataas jeeo |
lines.english: By the Pleasure of His Will, that Lord becomes my Husband, and I am imbued with the Praises of the Lord, the treasure of virtue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਣੈ ਸਹੁ ਭੀਹਾਵਲਾ ਹਉ ਆਵਣਿ ਜਾਣਿ ਮੁਈਆਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhaanai sahu bheehaavalaa hau aavan jaan mueeaas jeeo |
lines.english: By the Pleasure of Your Will, O my Husband Lord, I am afraid of You, and I come and go, and die.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਸਹੁ ਅਗਮੁ ਅਤੋਲਵਾ ਹਉ ਕਹਿ ਕਹਿ ਢਹਿ ਪਈਆਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: too sahu agam atolavaa hau keh keh dteh peeaas jeeo |
lines.english: You, O my Husband Lord, are inaccessible and immeasurable; talking and speaking of You, I have fallen at Your Feet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਮਾਗਉ ਕਿਆ ਕਹਿ ਸੁਣੀ ਮੈ ਦਰਸਨ ਭੂਖ ਪਿਆਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa maagau kiaa keh sunee mai darasan bhookh piaas jeeo |
lines.english: What should I beg for? What should I say and hear? I am hungry and thirsty for the Blessed Vision of Your Darshan.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਸਹੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਕੀ ਅਰਦਾਸਿ ਜੀਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gurasabadee sahu paaeaa sach naanak kee aradaas jeeo |2|
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Teachings, I have found my Husband Lord. This is Nanak's true prayer. ||2||
